titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 571

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=884 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.414606+00 | translation moa=885 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.427184+00

div.p:
David dei gratia Rex Scottorum Omnibus Sanctę ęcclesię fidelibus pręsentibus et futuris
salutem . Sciatis me dedissse et concessisse ęcclesię sanctę Marie . et Sancti Cuthberti de
Coldingaham . et Conuentui ibidem deseruienti  Ederham . et Nesebitam . in perpetuam elemosinam sicut Cospatricus frater Dalfini eas tenuit
die qua fuit uiuus et mortuus cum suis rectis diuisis . ita liberas et
quietas sicut alias terras tenent quę ad Coldingaham Pertinent . in ęcclesiis
et aquis et pratis . Presentibus testibus . Hereberto abbate . de Rochesburc .
Daniele priore de Gedwrda Ascelino archidiacono de Glasgv . Willelmo filio
Dvnecan . Dvnecano Comite Hvgone de Moreuilla . Geruasio Ridel . Alano filio
Waldeof . et Cospatric fratre suo . Willelmo de Sumeruilla . Willelmo de Graham .
Aput Rochesburc . xvii . kl . Septembris . Anno . M . C . xxx . ix .

p.cl: David, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all Holy Church's faithful, present and to come, greeting.

Know me to have given and conceded to the church of St Mary and St Cuthbert at Coldingham, and to the monastic community serving there, Edrom and Nisbet in perpetual alms, just as Cospatric the brother of Dolphin held them on the day he was alive and dead, with their correct boundaries, as free and quit as they hold other lands which belong to Coldingham, in churches, waters, and meadows.

Present as witnesses: Herbert the abbot of Roxburgh, Daniel the prior of Jedburgh, Ascelin the archdeacon of Glasgow, William the son of Duncan, Duncan the earl, Hugh de Morville, Gervase Ridel, Alan son of Waldeve, and Cospatric his own brother, William de Somerville, William of Graham.
At Roxburgh on the 17th day before the kalends of September [16th August] in the year 1139.
